name: @radzor/event-tracker
description: Track user events and page views for analytics. Buffers events in memory and flushes them in batches to a configurable HTTP endpoint. Zero dependencies — uses raw fetch.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: HTTP endpoint to POST event batches to.

inputs.name: batchSize
inputs.description: Number of events to buffer before auto-flushing.

inputs.name: flushIntervalMs
inputs.description: Max time in ms between automatic flushes.

inputs.name: headers
inputs.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with each flush (e.g. auth tokens).

fields.name: statusCode
fields.description: HTTP status code from the endpoint.

events.name: onEventTracked
events.description: Fired each time an event is added to the buffer.

events.name: onFlush
events.description: Fired when the event buffer is flushed to the endpoint.

actions.name: track

params.name: properties
params.description: Arbitrary key-value properties for the event.

actions.description: Track a custom event with optional properties.

actions.name: identify
actions.description: Associate subsequent events with a user identity.

params.name: url
params.description: URL or path of the page.

actions.name: flush
actions.description: Immediately flush all buffered events to the endpoint.

llm.constraints: Events are buffered in memory; if the process crashes before a flush, buffered events are lost. The endpoint must accept POST with a JSON array body. Uses fetch() — in Node.js < 18, a polyfill is required.
